points.point: All required sheets exist
Debt schedule models multiple tranches with interest, mandatory amortization and cash-flow sweep that reduces balances
3 statements are linked (net income -> retained earnings/BS, FCF -> cash -> BS, no plugged cash)
Returns computed from equity cash-flow series (entry equity out, exit equity in) — IRR/MoM via formulas, not hardcoded
Sensitivity table is a real 2-way data table (entry x exit), not static numbers
Assumptions centralized + named ranges + source notes on key inputs
No #REF!/circularity hacks; credit stats reference the debt schedule

quality.strengths: Model covers all major LBO components with centralized assumptions, named ranges, and notes. Debt schedule includes multiple tranches, mandatory amortization, and cash sweep mechanics. Returns use equity cash flows with IRR/MoM formulas.
quality.gaps: Sensitivity table is not a real 2-way data table (appears static). Balance sheet and cash flow statement are missing or incomplete, breaking full 3-statement linkage. Some sheets (e.g., Cover & Summary) may be merged, but full set not clearly separated.

points.point: Purchase price + premium computed from target shares/price; PPA produces goodwill and step-up
Financing split (cash vs stock) drives new debt, new shares issued, and incremental interest
Pro forma combined IS = BUY + TGT + synergies - new interest (after-tax), built with formulas referencing standalones
EPS accretion/dilution = PF EPS vs standalone acquirer EPS, for 3 years
Synergy phase-in and break-even synergy level addressed
Sensitivity of accretion vs %cash and vs synergies is a real data table
Outputs reference Assumptions (premium, %cash, rate, synergies) — no magic numbers

quality.strengths: Model includes clear assumptions sheet, transaction summary, sources & uses, PPA with goodwill and step-up, and pro forma IS with accretion/dilution over 3 years. Formulas generally reference assumptions and standalone IS data.
quality.gaps: Pro forma IS incorrectly includes TGT interest expense (should be retired) and lacks synergy phase-in logic. Sensitivity tables are missing (only labels present, no data table formulas). Break-even synergy analysis is absent.

points.point: CFADS built from EBITDA - capex - cash taxes - delta NWC, by year, via formulas
Cash sweep waterfall applies CFADS across tranches in priority and reduces balances year over year
All four covenant ratios computed each year referencing debt balances and EBITDA
Headroom vs each covenant limit shown; breach year flagged (e.g., conditional logic)
Scenario toggle (base/downside) actually changes EBITDA path and breach outcome
SOFR/spread used for interest; no hardcoded interest
Methodology notes on key cells

quality.strengths: No construction evidence provided; agent emitted empty summary.
quality.gaps: No sheets, cells, formulas, or Office.js snippets were built. All rubric points are unmet.

points.point: Monthly cohort retention triangle built
Retention/churn computed from signup_date vs churn_date logic
Churn rate monthly + annualized; LTV per plan uses MRR, churn and a discount factor
12-month MRR forecast combines surviving base + new adds
KPI dashboard with formulas
Conditional formatting applied to retention triangle
Handles censored rows correctly

quality.strengths: No construction evidence provided; agent emitted empty summary.
quality.gaps: No sheets, formulas, or logic were built. All rubric points are unmet.

points.point: Seasonal indices (12) derived from history and normalized (~average 1.0)
Trend estimated (moving average or regression) and combined with seasonality for the forecast
18-month forward forecast via formulas referencing trend + seasonal index
P10/P50/P90 scenario bands present and driven by an assumption (volatility/error)
Backtest on a 6-month holdout with MAPE/MAE computed by formula
Driver table (growth, seasonality) is editable and flows into the forecast
At least one chart of actual vs forecast

quality.strengths: Model includes full decomposition with seasonal indices, linear trend, and additive forecast. Backtest with MAE/MAPE and scenario bands (P10/P90) are formula-driven and linked to driver assumptions.
quality.gaps: No chart was created despite the requirement. Driver table includes trend parameters but lacks explicit editable seasonal factors; seasonality is fixed via VLOOKUP on a static table, not directly editable in the driver sheet.

points.point: Clean sheet: duplicates removed, dates normalized, numeric casts
Category normalization to canonical values via mapping
Join transactions to product cost/brand to compute margin
Pivot summaries: revenue by category x month, margin by product, top-10 products
KPI dashboard with conditional formatting
Cleaning rules documented in notes or a rules sheet
Original raw sheets left intact

quality.strengths: Clean sheet construction covers dedup, date parsing, numeric casts, and join; pivot summaries and KPI dashboard are structurally present with formulas; cleaning rules are documented in a dedicated section.
quality.gaps: Category normalization uses only PROPER() without a mapping table to canonical values; conditional formatting is mentioned but no evidence of actual rules applied; some formulas reference limited ranges (e.g., A2:H100) which may not scale.
